Prunus serotina. Elliptical, serrate leaves. Usually brown, downy hairs along bottom of midrib near petiole. Younger bark with horizontal lenticels. Older bark scaly. Planted 1998.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.

Cornus mas. Leaves similar to C. kousabut flowers are small and yellow; flowers somewhat similar to those on red-stemmed dogwood. Fruit 5/8" long; bright orange-red, turning darker as it matures; edible.
Corylus colurna. Large, toothed, simple, alternate leaves. Small nuts in burs held in large clusters. Pyramidal shape when young. Betulaceae family (birch, alder, etc.). Planted 2001.
Corylus maximavar. 'Purpurea'. Shrubby tree with large purple leaves. Good nuts.
[1x] Regal Prince Oak (Quercus robur x bicolor). Planted 24-Aug-09.

Quercus alba. Deep lobes; rounded ends. Warty acorn cap covering less than one third of the nut. Like all oaks in the white oak group, they germinate in the fall and have furry inner shells.
Quercus cerris. Leaves with fairly deep lobes; lobes pointed at tips. Acorn cap with elongated scales that make it look hairy or fringed, covering most of nut. Unusual tree. Planted 1999.
Quercus muehlenbergii. Leaves shallowly toothed rather than lobed. 1/2" to 3/4" long acorn with cap covering about 1/2 of nut. Planted 1996.
Quercus robur. Non-fastigiate form. Look for "earlobes" near petiole. Acorns with long peduncle (stalk).
Quercus macrocarpa. Look for ridges on twigs, hairy acorn cap. Shallow lobes on upper part of leaf; leaf hairy and lighter colored underneath. Tree has had heavy root damage. in 1990s.

Diospyros virginiana var. 'Meader'. Yellow to red fall color. Fruit a large, orange berry. Blocky older bark. Ebenaceae family. Planted 1996.
